Ref: {{ref}}
Board Resolution Package
ESOP Transaction — Corporate Resolutions Required
Company
{{companyName}}
Entity Type
{{entityType}}
Resolution Date
{{resolutionDate}}
Board Members
{{boardMembers}}
Note: These resolutions are templates and should be reviewed by corporate counsel before adoption. The specific language may need to be adapted to your state's corporate statute and your company's bylaws.
Res. 01Authorize Exploration of ESOP

RESOLVED, that the Board of Directors of {{companyName}} hereby authorizes the exploration of an Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) as a potential transaction structure for the company, and directs management to engage qualified advisors to evaluate the feasibility, structure, and tax implications of such a transaction.

FURTHER RESOLVED, that the Board authorizes management to share confidential company information with potential ESOP advisors, trustees, and counsel for the purpose of obtaining preliminary assessments and proposals, subject to the execution of appropriate non-disclosure agreements.

Res. 02Engage Forhemit as Transaction Advisor

RESOLVED, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the engagement of Forhemit Transition Stewardship ("Forhemit") as the company's exclusive transaction advisor for the proposed ESOP transition, on the terms set forth in the Engagement Letter dated between the company and Forhemit.

FURTHER RESOLVED, that the officers of the company are hereby authorized and directed to execute the Engagement Letter and any related documents, and to take such further action as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the intent of this resolution.

Res. 03Authorize Letter of Intent Execution

RESOLVED, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes {{sellerName}} or another designated officer to negotiate, execute, and deliver a Letter of Intent ("LOI") with the ESOP Trustee for the sale of the company's stock to an ESOP trust, on terms substantially consistent with the summary provided to the Board, including but not limited to:

a.Purchase price within the range of the independent valuation;
b.Transaction structure involving 100% acquisition by the ESOP;
c.Payment terms including cash at closing and a seller note;
d.Exclusivity period of 90 days from LOI execution;
e.Conditions precedent including financing, appraisal, and ERISA compliance.
Res. 04Authorize Entity Conversion
{{#if entityType}}

RESOLVED, that if required for the ESOP transaction, the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the conversion of {{companyName}} from its current entity form to a C-Corporation organized under the laws of the state of incorporation, and authorizes the officers of the company to:

{{else}}

RESOLVED, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the conversion of the company to a C-Corporation, if required, and authorizes the officers to:

{{/if}}
a.File articles of conversion and articles of incorporation with the applicable state authorities;
b.Make the "check-the-box" election (Form 8832) to be treated as a C-Corporation for federal tax purposes;
c.Obtain a new EIN if required;
d.Update all contracts, licenses, permits, and registrations to reflect the new entity;
e.Take such further action as may be necessary to complete the conversion.
Important: Entity conversion may trigger built-in gains tax if the company has appreciated assets. Corporate counsel and the company's CPA should model the tax impact before this resolution is adopted.
Res. 05Authorize ESOP Plan Adoption

RESOLVED, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes and approves the adoption of the {{companyName}} Employee Stock Ownership Plan and Trust (the "ESOP"), in substantially the form presented to the Board, and authorizes the officers of the company to:

a.Execute the ESOP Plan document and Trust Agreement;
b.Appoint the initial ESOP Trustee;
c.Establish the ESOP trust bank account and fund the initial contribution;
d.Authorize the company to borrow funds to finance the ESOP acquisition and to guarantee or collateralize such borrowings;
e.Authorize the company to make contributions to the ESOP sufficient to service the acquisition debt;
f.File the initial Form 5500 and all required regulatory filings;
g.Engage an ESOP administrator, recordkeeper, and investment advisor as needed.
—Certification

I, the undersigned Secretary of {{companyName}}, do hereby certify that the foregoing resolutions were duly adopted by the Board of Directors at a meeting duly called and held on {{resolutionDate}}, at which a quorum was present and acting throughout.

Secretary Name (Print)
Secretary Signature
Date
Attest
